questions.question: What is the core principle of Behavioral Activation?

options.text: Wait until you feel motivated, then take action
Schedule and take action first, even without motivation - feeling better follows action
Only engage in activities that feel effortless
Focus only on pleasurable activities, ignoring responsibilities

questions.explanation: The core principle of Behavioral Activation is 'action before motivation.' Traditional thinking says feel motivated → act → feel better. BA reverses this: schedule action → take action → feel better → build motivation. You don't wait to feel like doing something.

questions.question: What is the '5-Minute Rule' in Behavioral Activation?

options.text: You must exercise for exactly 5 minutes
Commit to just 5 minutes of any activity - starting is the hardest part, and you can stop after 5 minutes if you want
Take a 5-minute break every hour
Wait 5 minutes before starting any task

questions.explanation: The 5-Minute Rule helps overcome the inertia of starting. Commit to just 5 minutes of any activity, with permission to stop after 5 minutes. This reduces overwhelm, and most people find that once started, they often want to continue. Starting is the highest friction point.

questions.question: What are the five categories of activities in Behavioral Activation?

options.text: Work, rest, eat, sleep, and exercise
Pleasure, Mastery, Social, Physical, and Values-based
Morning, afternoon, evening, night, and weekend
Easy, medium, hard, very hard, and impossible

questions.explanation: The five activity categories are: Pleasure (enjoyment), Mastery (accomplishment), Social (connection), Physical (movement/energy), and Values-based (meaning/purpose). A balanced life includes activities from all categories.

questions.question: How effective is Behavioral Activation compared to full Cognitive Therapy, according to research?

options.text: Much less effective
No significant difference - BA is as effective as full cognitive therapy
Slightly more effective but much harder to implement
Only effective for mild cases

questions.explanation: Research shows Behavioral Activation has no significant difference from full Cognitive Therapy (g = 0.02) in effectiveness, while being simpler to implement. BA produces large effects (g = 0.87) compared to control groups and is endorsed as a first-line treatment.

questions.question: When tracking activities and mood, what should you look for after several days?

options.text: Only focus on activities that felt perfect
Which activities consistently boost mood, which drain it, and any gaps in activity categories
How many hours you spent in bed
Whether you felt motivated before each activity

questions.explanation: When reviewing your activity-mood log, look for: mood boosters (which activities consistently improve mood), mood drains (which lower mood), gaps in categories (are you missing social? physical?), and patterns (certain times better or worse?). This data helps you plan strategically.

title: Getting Moving Again: Behavioral Activation Basics - Quiz
